citations.description: Ticket has zero comments — no run attestation anywhere.

findings.criterion: make lint && make typecheck && make test green.
findings.detail: This criterion requires executing the full test/lint/typecheck suite and confirming it passes. The ticket has no comments and no recorded attestation of a passing run. While the implementation changes look syntactically clean and the three targeted tests are fixed, we cannot verify suite-wide pass/fail from static inspection alone — and the codebase contains pre-existing RED MARKER tests (test_build_dep_graph_single_batch_scan, test_find_direct_blockers_no_per_ticket_scan, test_compute_dep_graph_children_use_preloaded_state in test_graph_perf.py lines 108–238, and tests in test_graph_children_archive.py) that carry no @pytest.mark.xfail and appear intended to fail until related ticket 19d7 is implemented. These would cause `make test` to report failures. No ticket comment attests to a green run.
findings.remediation: Run `make lint && make typecheck && make test` in a provisioned environment and record the outcome (pass/fail, any pre-existing RED marker exclusions) as a ticket comment including the run timestamp and the command output summary.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
